steps.title: Parabola opens upward and line has positive slope
steps.content: The displayed parabola opens upward, so the quadratic coefficient must be positive. The displayed line has positive slope, so the linear coefficient must also be positive. The matching equations are $y=\frac{7}{16}x^2$ and $y=\frac{7}{4}x$.

steps.title: Verify with the intersection point
steps.content: At the visible intersection point $(4, 7)$, the remaining alternative checks out: the parabola gives $y = \frac{7}{16}(4)^2 = \frac{7}{16} \cdot 16 = 7$, and the line gives $y = \frac{7}{4}(4) = 7$. That matches the correct answer, D.
